steps.title: Find the defining moment about each person
steps.content: The key signal is the single phrase: “sat down with the feeling that, for some reason which he could not grasp, his strokes had gone awry.” This reveals that each speaker experiences a failure—their attempt to express their artistic vision doesn't work (strokes had gone awry)—yet cannot understand why (could not grasp the reason).

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: B names the exact two-part failure: incoherent presentation plus ignorance of why—matching “strokes had gone awry” with “could not grasp” the reason. A misreads the struggle as contempt, C invents a debate from one-way speeches and private clarifications, and D wrongly claims public delight paired with private detestation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
